parsed_review.summary: CP00-170 is a clean, descriptor-only continuation pack consistent with the established CP15x-CP169 pattern. Verified: (1) No side effects in package code - createMasterDataFailureTaxonomyEdgeCaseEscalationDescriptor/Catalog and the Hermes/Claude/handoff builders only assemble frozen descriptors, run JSON.stringify leak checks, and reuse CP169's pure taxonomy descriptor builder; freezeFailureTaxonomyEdgeCaseEscalationResult and MASTER_DATA_CP170_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION pin all no-write flags false (no real-data load, runtime permission eval, audit append, route/approval dispatch, API handler, network, UI/DOM, AI retrieval, Hermes/Claude execution, retry/rollback, lock acquisition, or case-note writes). (2) CP169 failure taxonomy source coverage is preserved - failure_recovery_bindings rebuild source descriptors via createMasterDataPermissionAuditWorkflowFailureTaxonomyDescriptor (test confirms source pack_id CP00-169), and the two edge-case descriptors (Claude edge-case prompt, human escalation note) are added descriptor-only with sends_claude_prompt/dispatches_review_route/dispatches_approval_route/writes_case_note all false. (3) Customer-facing summaries exclude all prohibited internal data - leak_guard internal_reference_fields and prohibited_outputs are enforced per-row and at catalog level (customer_surface_excludes_internal_refs, prohibited_output_absent), keeping permission refs, audit internals, blocked-claim refs, foreign tenant IDs, hidden source values, rule candidates, stale payloads, internal prompts/notes, and reviewer identity off the renderable surface. (4) Coverage, Hermes packet, Claude packet, handoff, and contract parity are gated by validateMasterDataCp170Coverage, validateMasterDataCp170FailureTaxonomyEdgeCaseEscalation (with contractProjection JSON parity), the Hermes/Claude/handoff builders, model.test, and the validation script (which also bumps schema v0.14->v0.15 and re-points all current/upstream/next bindings); distributions sum to 40 and contract key ordering matches the registry for stringify parity. (5) No HRX split or LDIP implementation is introduced (implements_ldip/splits_hrx_product false; README no-effect boundary explicit). Three non-blocking P3 advisories noted (internal taxonomy key on customer summary, validator no-write subset vs. script-level full coverage, hard-coded "Risk A" handoff label). Gate: pass.

boundary_assessment.notes: CP00-170 builds frozen edge-case escalation descriptors only; package code does not load real data, execute runtime permission checks, audit appends, route dispatch, API handlers, UI rendering, AI retrieval, analytics, Hermes, Claude, retry, rollback, locks, or case-note writes.

findings.title: Customer-facing edge-case summary carries internal failure-taxonomy category key
findings.description: In createMasterDataFailureTaxonomyEdgeCaseEscalationDescriptor, customerFacingEdgeCaseSummary includes source_descriptor_key (e.g. "cross_tenant_failure", "permission_denied_failure") for customer_visible bindings. This is an internal CP169 failure-taxonomy category label. It is not in leak_guard.internal_reference_fields or prohibited_outputs, so it passes customer_surface_excludes_internal_refs and prohibited_output_absent, and it is a generic category rather than a permission ref, rule, payload, or tenant ID, so no real leak occurs. Given the strict customer-surface boundary, exposing internal taxonomy keys on the renderable summary is worth confirming as intentional.
findings.recommendation: Confirm exposing source_descriptor_key on the customer surface is intended; if customer surfaces should carry only safe_error_code/outcome, drop source_descriptor_key from customerFacingEdgeCaseSummary while retaining it in internal_edge_case_evidence.
findings.post_review_disposition: addressed: customer_facing_edge_case_summary no longer exposes source_descriptor_key; the source descriptor key remains in internal_edge_case_evidence only.

findings.title: Dedicated edge-case validator asserts only a subset of no-write attestation flags
findings.description: validateMasterDataCp170FailureTaxonomyEdgeCaseEscalation directly asserts loads_real_client_data, evaluates_runtime_permission, appends_audit_event, dispatches_review_route, dispatches_approval_route, executes_ai_retrieval, emits_hermes_evidence, sends_claude_prompt, and writes_case_note, but not executes_hermes_command, executes_claude_review, executes_retry, executes_rollback, acquires_runtime_lock, renders_ui, mutates_dom, or issues_network_request. Those flags are still gated because scripts/validate-rp04-master-data-contract.mjs iterates every MASTER_DATA_CP170_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION key against the contract, so full coverage exists. This is defense-in-depth, not a coverage gap.
findings.recommendation: Optionally mirror the full no-write flag set inside the dedicated validator so the package-level gate is self-contained and not solely reliant on the contract-parity script.
findings.post_review_disposition: addressed: validateMasterDataCp170FailureTaxonomyEdgeCaseEscalation now mirrors additional no-write/no-effect flags including Hermes/Claude execution, retry/rollback, runtime locks, UI/DOM, network, API, and product writes.

findings.title: Closeout handoff hard-codes CP00-171 as Risk A
findings.description: createMasterDataCp170CloseoutHandoff sets open_scope to a free-text string describing CP00-171 / RP04.P07.M05.S19 as a "Risk A" failure taxonomy boundary. This forward-looking classification is not verifiable from this diff and is not cross-checked against the closeout plan; if CP00-171's risk class differs, the handoff text would be misleading.
findings.recommendation: Verify CP00-171's risk class against docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json (or derive it) rather than hard-coding "Risk A" in the handoff string.
findings.post_review_disposition: addressed: createMasterDataCp170CloseoutHandoff no longer hard-codes the next-pack risk class in open_scope.
